name: PlanetScale API
what_it_does: PlanetScale's REST API for managing MySQL-compatible serverless databases with Git-like branching for schema changes, deployments, and team collaboration.
use_cases: Managing database branches for schema migrations via agents
Creating deploy requests to merge schema changes to production
Monitoring database performance metrics and query insights
Provisioning new databases and branches programmatically
Managing database users and passwords for application access
not_for: Teams needing PostgreSQL (PlanetScale is MySQL only)
Applications requiring foreign key constraints (PlanetScale disables them for Vitess)
Simple hobby projects (pricing changed, no longer has a free tier)
Teams needing on-premise or self-hosted MySQL
best_when: An agent needs to manage PlanetScale database branches and schema deployments as part of a database DevOps workflow.
avoid_when: You need PostgreSQL, foreign key constraints, or a free tier for experimentation.

auth.notes: Service tokens with configurable permissions per organization/database. Simple bearer token auth. Scopes include database creation, branching, and deploy request management.

pricing.paid_tiers: Scaler $39/database/month, Scaler Pro custom, Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: PlanetScale removed free tier in 2024. Now requires paid plan from the start. Pricing based on rows read/written per month.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented for management API

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API errors are well-structured with error and message fields. OpenAPI spec makes it easy to generate clients. Error messages are generally clear.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Branch creation is idempotent by name. Deploy requests track state, preventing duplicate deployments.
known_agent_gotchas: No free tier since 2024 — cannot test without a paid plan
Foreign key constraints disabled — agents must handle referential integrity in application code
Schema changes require branching workflow: create branch, make changes, open deploy request, merge
No official SDK — agents must use raw HTTP or generate from OpenAPI spec
Deploy requests can fail if schema changes conflict — agents need to handle deploy failures
Rows read/written billing model can be expensive for read-heavy agent workloads
